INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: 300% more traffic is observed compared to past window average traffic - 28264.0;System: CV9V ,DC: AWS-E ,MetricName: traffic ,Application: SOE-Static-Akamai-ASSETS.example.com
CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.

INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: db error count >= 15.0;System:EV6V, DC:AWS-E , MetricName: db error count ,Application: CXP-Aggregate-CXP-Orderanalyzer-Batch

CLOSURE_REMARKS: Hard throughput limit (Permitted Throughput) equal to the calculated baseline limit for EFS filesystem in AWS-E. Bursting mode has the potential to cause this issue we only recommend it for non-critical workloads. You may consider using Provisioned Throughput mode or Elastic Throughput mode instead. Restarted port on 2199 on tpswpsgnldp002. Now port is up and running fine. The actual issue was on AWS-E EFS filesystem on EC2 instance hosting replication from OnPrem to AWS-E. This incident in Onprem is a victim of the issue in AWS-E.

INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: 300% more traffic is observed compared to past window average traffic - 26019.0;System: CV9V ,DC: AWS-E ,MetricName: traffic ,Application: SOE-Static-Akamai-ASSETS.example.com
CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.

INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: 300% more traffic is observed compared to past window average traffic - 28832.0;System: CV9V ,DC: AWS-E ,MetricName: traffic ,Application: SOE-Static-Akamai-ASSETS.example.com
CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.
